attacking_style: Excels from long-range breakaways that form after sustained high-speed racing (70km+ escapes after 55kph starts). Struggles with finale timing, attacking too early or misjudging optimal launch distance.
team_tactics: Willing to prioritize personal ambition over team objectives (attacked from break despite defending teammate's jersey). Limited evidence of structured team support for his moves.
terrain_preference: Selective, reduced-bunch finales that favor strong breakaway riders with finishing speed. Best suited to stages that thin peloton through attrition rather than pure climbing.
vulnerability: Tactical inexperience in finales—poor distance judgment and premature attacking. Form appears inconsistent year-to-year, unable to replicate breakthrough performances on similar terrain.
key_patterns: Breakaway specialist requiring large groups (25 riders) to succeed, not solo efforts
Fast finisher within reduced groups, fastest sprint in 2022 Giro breakaway finale
Tactical misjudgment in closing kilometers across multiple observations (too early attacks)
Form regression between seasons—2022 breakthrough not sustained into 2023 on comparable stages
form_trajectory: negative: 2023 Giro showed inability to replicate 2022 winning performance on similar selective terrain, indicating form decline rather than continued development
